title: 10 best herbal creams for feverish feeling

remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activity: Purple coneflower contains compounds like quercetin and kaempferol, which have been shown to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, helping to alleviate symptoms of fever such as pain and discomfort.

Immune-modulating properties: The active constituents in purple coneflower, including triterpenoid saponins, may help stimulate the immune system to combat infections that cause fever, while also reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.

Soothing and cooling effects: The topical application of purple coneflower cream can provide rapid relief from feverish feelings by reducing skin temperature, soothing heat rash or sunburn, and calming irritated skin.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, creams helps with feverish feeling because of its immune-boosting properties. The cream is extracted from the root of the plant, which contains alkylamides, phenolic acids, and glycoproteins that work together to stimulate the immune system. This stimulation helps to fight off infections and reduce the severity of fever. As a result, the feverish feeling subsides, and the body's natural defenses are strengthened, allowing it to recover faster from illnesses. Its anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe and calm the body.
remedy_constituents: Icariin: A flavonoid glycoside, icariin has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the body's temperature response to infections, thus alleviating feverish feelings.
Alkylamides: These terpenoid compounds have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the body's immune response and reduce the severity of fever.
Cichoric acid: A phenolic compound, cichoric acid has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help combat infections causing fever and reduce inflammation in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are widely used due to their high concentration of alkylamides, which have been shown to have immunomodulatory effects and help alleviate feverish feelings.
Leaves: Echinacea leaves contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them suitable for creams targeting feverish feelings.
Flowers: Echinacea flowers are rich in flavonoids, saponins, and volatile oils, which may help in reducing inflammation and fever, and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 30 grams of dried echinacea purpurea flowers in 300 ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax with 10 grams of shea butter and 5 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Gradually add 20 ml of the echinacea infusion to the melted wax mixture and stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into small containers and allow it to cool and harden at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol has a cooling effect on the skin, which helps to reduce body temperature and alleviate hot flashes associated with fever.
Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol is a natural decongestant that helps to clear mucus from the airways, making it easier for the body to expel excess heat and toxins during a fever.
Vitamin A: Vitamin A has antiviral properties, which help to combat the underlying infection causing the fever, while also supporting immune function and reducing inflammation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with feverish feeling because of its natural cooling and soothing properties. The menthol content in peppermint helps to cool the skin, providing relief from heat and discomfort. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce swelling and ease congestion, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation. As a result, peppermint creams can help to alleviate the discomfort and discomfort associated with feverish feelings, promoting a sense of well-being and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol helps to cool the body and relieve fever by causing a sensation of coldness when applied topically.
Menthone: Menthone has a mild antipyretic effect, helping to reduce fever by acting on the hypothalamus, the part of the brain that regulates body temperature.

Catechins (phenolic compounds): Catechins, such as epicatechin and epigallocatechin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate fever and reduce inflammation associated with feverish conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their analgesic and antipyretic properties, which help reduce fever and alleviate associated pain.
Roots: Contain compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and fever, making them a popular choice for fever-reducing creams.
Stems: Similar to leaves, stems are used for their anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which help in managing feverish conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves at the peak of the plant's potency and fragrance.
Combine the fresh leaves with 2 cups of distilled water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 10 to 15 minutes to release its oils.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Whip 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/2 cup of coconut oil into the cooled herbal infusion to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol's cooling and anaesthetic properties provide rapid relief from feverish sensations, such as itching, burning, or stinging skin, by numbing the affected area.

Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a compound found in licorice extract, helps to reduce inflammation and fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting anti-inflammatory responses.

Methyl salicylate: Methyl salicylate, also known as wintergreen oil, has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help to relieve pain, reduce swelling, and lower body temperature, making it an effective natural alternative to aspirin or ibuprofen.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gaultheria procumbens, also known as wintergreen, creams helps with feverish feeling because of its unique properties. The primary compound, methyl salicylate, has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that soothe and calm the body. This herbal cream also contains antioxidants and other bioactive compounds that promote relaxation and reduce discomfort associated with fever. By applying the cream topically, the ingredients are absorbed directly into the skin, providing rapid relief from symptoms, and helping to alleviate the overall feeling of being under the weather.

Gaultherin: Gaultherin has anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which can help to reduce body temperature and alleviate pain and discomfort associated with fever.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make creams for feverish feeling because they contain compounds like salicin and salicylic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.

Buds: They are used to make creams for feverish feeling because they contain aromatic oils and flavonoids, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of dried gaultheria procumbens leaves and flowers from the plant at dawn when they are still moist.
Steep the dried gaultheria procumbens in 2 cups of boiling water for 5 minutes to create a strong infusion.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth into a bowl and discard the solids after 5 minutes of steeping.
Mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of shea butter in a double boiler.
Add 2 tablespoons of the gaultheria procumbens infusion to the melted beeswax mixture and stir until well combined.

remedy_properties: Antipyretic property: Lemon balm cream has been shown to reduce fever by dilating blood vessels, allowing heat to escape from the body and reducing inflammation, making it an effective natural remedy for fever relief.
Anxiolytic property: Lemon balm's calming effects can help alleviate feelings of anxiety and restlessness that often accompany feverish sensations, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.

Anti-inflammatory property: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds may help soothe and reduce inflammation caused by fever, providing relief from discomfort, soreness, and redness associated with high temperatures.

The active compounds in Melissa officinalis, such as rosmarinic acid and tannins, work to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, thereby alleviating the discomfort associated with fever. The soothing and calming effects of Melissa officinalis creams also help to ease restlessness and promote a good night's sleep, which is essential for the body to recover from fever.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate feverish feelings.

Oleanolic acid: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate feverish feelings, in addition to its ability to stabilize cell membranes and maintain the body's temperature.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make creams due to their high content of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties.
Flowers: They are used to make creams due to their soothing and calming effects, as well as their ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.

Buds: They are used to make creams due to their rich content of essential oils and bioactive compounds, which have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties that help to alleviate feverish feelings.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves in late morning when the dew has evaporated and the sun is not intense.
Steam distill the harvested leaves to obtain the essential oil which requires a 2-3 hour preparation time.
Combine 20 grams of melissa officinalis essential oil with 100 grams of a vegetable base oil in a mixing bowl.
Blend the mixture for 10 minutes to ensure uniform distribution and then filter the cream using a cheesecloth.
Transfer the herbal cream into sterilized containers and store them in a refrigerator at 4-8 degrees celsius temperature.

remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine, a key constituent of goldenseal, has anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and lower body temperature, alleviating feverish sensations.

Isoquinoline alkaloids: The isoquinoline alkaloids present in goldenseal cream, such as berberastine, possess antimicrobial and antiviral activities, helping to combat infections that can cause fevers.

Flavonoids and lignans: Goldenseal's flavonoids and lignans have antioxidant properties, which help protect the body from oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by fever, while also supporting immune function.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hydrastis canadensis, also known as goldenseal, creams helps with feverish feeling because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream contains berberine, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and fight off infections, helping to alleviate feverish sensations. Additionally, goldenseal's antiviral properties can help combat the underlying cause of the fever, promoting a sense of relief and comfort. remedy_constituents: Alkaloid (Hydrastine): As a natural antimicrobial agent, hydrastine helps reduce fever by inhibiting the growth of pathogens that can cause infection and contribute to fever.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are primarily used due to their high concentration of hydrastine, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, being a part of the root system, also contain hydrastine, making them a valuable source for fever-reducing creams.
Leaves: The leaves are used as they contain berberine, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties that can help alleviate feverish feelings.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25 grams of dried hydrastis canadensis root and 15 grams of beeswax in the morning.
Weigh 200 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 100 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the oil mixture for 30 minutes at 150 degrees fahrenheit to melt the beeswax.
Stir in 25 grams of dried hydrastis canadensis root into the oil mixture for 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into glass jars and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedy_properties: Allantoin: Allantoin has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with fevers, allowing the body to focus on recovering from illness.
Glucoalkaloids: Glucoalkaloids in comfrey have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, which can help combat infection-causing pathogens and reduce feverish symptoms like chills and sweats.

remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part for making creams to alleviate feverish feelings due to their high concentration of mucilages, which provide soothing and anti-inflammatory properties.

Flowers: The flowers are used in creams to help reduce fever and alleviate other symptoms due to their ability to promote sweating, which can aid in the release of toxins from the body.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried symphytum officinale roots and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Macerate the herb in 100g of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container to separate the oil and solids.
Add 5g of beeswax to the oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler to dissolve the wax.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 30 minutes before transferring it to a jar.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and terpenes, which help to reduce swelling and relieve discomfort associated with fever.

Tannins: These polyphenolic compounds have a cooling effect and help to reduce fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh leaves and stems of urtica dioica and dry them thoroughly in a warm oven at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 1 cup of dried urtica dioica with 1 cup of olive oil in a clean glass jar and let it infuse for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the liquid to a saucepan.
Combine 1 cup of the infused oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil in the saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat while stirring constantly until the beeswax melts and the mixture reaches a smooth consistency.

remedy_properties: Tannins: Tannins help to reduce inflammation, ease pain, and lower body temperature by constricting blood vessels and reducing blood flow to affected areas.
Volatile Oils: Volatile oils in Joe Pye Weed creams have a cooling effect when applied topically, which can help to reduce heat and discomfort associated with feverish feelings.

remedy_recipe: Harvest eupatorium perfoliatum flowers and leaves in their optimal growing stage typically around late summer early fall.
Steam distill the plant material in a ratio of 1 part plant to 5 parts solvent for 3 hours.
Infuse the distillate in a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a 1 5 ratio for 2 weeks.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then add a thickening agent like beeswax or candelilla wax.
Mix the emulsion well and pour it into glass jars for a final product that is shelf stable for up to 2 years.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers at the peak of their potency.
Steep the dried flowers in 500 milliliters of carrier oil at 60 degrees celsius for 4 hours.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a clear extract.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax with 20 grams of shea butter and 10 milliliters of the matricaria extract.
Whip the mixture for 10 minutes until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency suitable for application.

Antimicrobial properties: Fennel cream's antimicrobial properties help to combat infection-causing bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can contribute to feverish feelings, reducing the risk of further complications and promoting recovery.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves and roots in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Infuse 2 cups of dried foeniculum vulgare in 4 cups of cold-pressed oil for 4 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container to separate the solids from the liquid.
Mix 2 ounces of beeswax with 2 ounces of shea butter to create a thickening agent for the cream.
Combine 8 ounces of the infused oil with the beeswax mixture and whip until smooth and creamy consistency is achieved.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with feverish feeling because of its inherent ability to soothe and calm the skin. The gel-like substance within aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling, providing instant relief from heat and discomfort. Additionally, aloe's antiseptic and antibacterial qualities work to clear pores and prevent infection, allowing the body to focus on fighting off the underlying cause of the fever, rather than dealing with skin irritation. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal aloe creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which can help reduce inflammation in the body, alleviating symptoms associated with fever such as pain, swelling, and redness.

Antioxidants: Aloe vera contains a wealth of antioxidants, including vitamins C and E, beta-carotene, and other polyphenols, which can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to fever-induced oxidative stress and damage.

Cooling and soothing agents: The gel extracted from the aloe leaf contains cooling and soothing compounds like mucilages, which can help calm the skin and reduce heat, providing relief from feverish sensations and discomfort.

Leaves: Aloe leaves contain a thick gel-like substance rich in vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that provide natural cooling and soothing properties, making them effective in reducing fever symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and wash 100g of aloe vera gel and mix it with 20g of echinacea extract.
Combine the mixture with 30ml of distilled water and heat it at 50°c for 15 minutes.
Add 10ml of glycerin and 5ml of vitamin e oil to the mixture while stirring constantly.
Allow the mixture to cool down to room temperature before whipping it into a cream consistency.
Transfer the herbal aloe vera cream to sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 30 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with feverish feeling because of its antipyretic properties, which help to reduce body temperature. remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh calendula officinalis flowers and rinse them gently with distilled water.
Combine the dried flowers with 200ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil at a ratio of 1:2.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes to allow the flowers to steep and infuse their properties.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Mix 50g of beeswax with 100ml of the infused oil and heat in a double boiler for an additional 10 minutes until smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary creams helps with feverish feeling because of its natural cooling properties. Stimulating and warming properties: Rosemary's stimulating and warming effects, courtesy of its camphor content, help increase blood circulation, which can help bring down a fever by dilating blood vessels and releasing heat.

remedy_recipe: Dry the fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves by spreading them out in a single layer for 7-10 days.
Grind 20 grams of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Combine the powdered rosmarinus officinalis with 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mixture reaches a temperature of 180 degrees fahrenheit.
Allow the cooled mixture to infuse for 2-3 hours before straining and filling into glass jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with feverish feeling because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and ease pain. Buds: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, they help to calm down the inflammatory response associated with fever, while also providing gentle antiseptic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots and flowers weighing about 100g, washing them gently under cold running water.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm oven at 50°c for 24 hours or use a food dehydrator at 60°c for 6-8 hours.
Combine 20g of dried dandelion root and flower with 40ml of carrier oil such as olive or coconut oil, macerating it for 48 hours.
Filter the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the plant material from the infused oil, discarding the solids.
Mix the infused oil with 10g of beeswax and 10ml of distilled water, heating gently over low heat until melted and blended, taking approximately 15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry creams helps with feverish feeling because of its unique combination of herbs that work together to ease inflammation and reduce body temperature. The cream's active ingredients, such as salicin and flavonoids, have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the skin and relax muscles, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating fever-induced discomfort. Additionally, the cream's cooling effect helps to bring down the body temperature, providing rapid relief from chills and sweats associated with fever.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: The herbal extracts in highbush cranberry creams, such as salicylic acid and polyphenols, help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of fever, including body aches and joint pain.

Antioxidant properties: Highbush cranberries contain powerful antioxidants that neutralize free radicals, which can contribute to oxidative stress and exacerbate fever-related symptoms like fatigue and weakness.

Immunomodulatory properties: The herbal extracts in highbush cranberry creams, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to modulate the immune response, helping to regulate cytokine production and reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate feverish feelings.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help soothe and calm the body's response to fevers.
Barks: The barks contain tannins, which have a cooling effect on the body, helping to reduce inflammation and ease feverish sensations.
remedy_recipe: Measure 20 grams of dried vaccinium macrocarpon leaves and add them to a heat-resistant container.
Combine 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil with the dried herb.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over simmering water for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another container leaving behind any sediment.
Allow the infused carrier oil to cool and then mix with beeswax and vitamin e oil as a thickening agent.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram creams helps with feverish feeling because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The herb contains compounds like thymol and borneol, which help to reduce body temperature, ease congestion, and alleviate headaches associated with fever. Additionally, marjoram's calming and soothing effects can promote relaxation and reduce stress, making it easier for the body to recover from illness. When applied topically as a cream, wild marjoram's active ingredients are absorbed quickly into the skin, providing fast relief from feverish symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic: Wild marjoram cream contains compounds like rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to exhibit antipyretic properties, helping to reduce fever and alleviate associated symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, help soothe inflammation and discomfort caused by fever, reducing redness, swelling, and pain.

Antiviral and antibacterial: Wild marjoram's antiviral and antibacterial activities may help combat underlying infections that can cause fever, preventing their spread and promoting the body's natural healing process.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and soothing feverish symptoms.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and terpenes, which help to calm down inflammation and reduce body temperature.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh origanum vulgare leaves measure out two cups of the leaves.
Dry the origanum vulgare leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for six hours.
Combine dried origanum vulgare leaves with one cup of sweet almond oil and four tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over medium heat for two hours or until it reaches 180 degrees fahrenheit then strain and cool.
Transfer the cooled cream to an airtight container and store it in the refrigerator for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with feverish feeling because they contain anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help to soothe and calm the skin. The cooling sensation of the cream can also help to reduce heat and alleviate discomfort associated with fever. Additionally, the herbal ingredients in elder creams may help to stimulate blood flow and promote detoxification, which can aid in the body's natural response to fighting off infection and recovering from illness. This combination of properties makes elder creams a popular natural remedy for relieving feverish symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antiviral compounds: Elder cream contains antiviral compounds like flavonoids, iridoid glycosides, and anthocyanins that help combat viral infections that cause fever and alleviate symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce inflammation and ease discomfort associated with fevers, such as headaches, body aches, and joint pain.

Cooling and soothing effects: Elder cream's cooling and soothing properties, often due to its high water content and the presence of glycerin, provide relief from fever-induced heat and itching sensations, making it an effective topical treatment for skin irritations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, such as alkaloids and glycosides, which help reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with feverish conditions.
Bark: Contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in relieving body aches and pains associated with fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in late summer or early fall when they are fully bloomed.
Dry the sambucus nigra material by spreading it out in a single layer on paper bags or trays for 7-10 days.
Combine 20 grams of dried sambucus nigra with 100 ml of cold-pressed coconut oil and infuse for 4-6 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the herbal solids and discard the residue.
Mix the strained oil with 50 grams of beeswax and heat until melted, then pour into glass jars and allow cooling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with feverish feeling because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The thymol present in thyme has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with fever, such as headaches and body aches. Additionally, the cream's cooling sensation can help to soothe and calm the skin, providing relief from the intense heat and discomfort often experienced during a fever. As the cream is absorbed into the skin, it helps to promote relaxation and reduces the body's natural response to inflammation, making it an effective way to alleviate feverish feelings.
remedy_properties: Thymol: Thymol, a compound found in thyme, has antipyretic properties, which means it helps to reduce fever by cooling the body and reducing inflammation.

Essential Oils: The essential oils present in thyme, such as carvacrol and borneol, have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help relieve symptoms of feverish feelings like headaches, muscle aches, and sweating.

Antiviral Properties: Thyme has been shown to exhibit antiviral properties, which can help combat the underlying viral infections that may be causing feverish feelings, reducing the duration and severity of illness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have been traditionally used to treat fever due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent infection.
Barks: The barks of thyme plants contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that act as natural antipyretics, helping to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms of fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves and dry them using air circulation or desiccation methods for 2 hours.
Grind the dried thyme into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, measuring out 20g of powder per batch.
Combine the thyme powder with 50ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a heat-resistant glass container, heating it gently over low heat for 15 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it steep at room temperature for 2 hours, allowing the thyme to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and transferring the cream to sterilized jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with feverish feeling because of its calming properties that soothe the body and mind. The sweet, floral scent of lavender has a natural ability to reduce anxiety and stress, which are common companions to fever. Additionally, the cream's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds help to cool and calm the skin, providing relief from heat and discomfort associated with fever. As the cream is absorbed, it promotes relaxation and calms the body, helping to alleviate feverish feelings.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial Properties: Lavender's antimicrobial properties help to reduce bacterial growth, which can contribute to fever and discomfort, by inhibiting the spread of infections.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool, help to reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin, alleviating symptoms associated with feverish feelings like headaches and joint pain.

Calming and Sedative Effects: Lavender's calming and sedative properties have a direct impact on the nervous system, helping to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety and stress, which can exacerbate feverish feelings.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming skin irritations associated with fevers.
Seeds: Lavender seeds contain a compound called linalool, which has natural antispasmodic and sedative effects, helping to relieve muscle cramps and tension caused by fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried lavender flowers according to standard botanical practices weighing approximately 20 grams.
Combine the dried lavender flowers with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.
Allow the mixture to steep and cool before straining it through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar.
Mix one tablespoon of beeswax with 20 grams of coconut oil and add it to the cooled lavender infused oil blend heating gently until fully incorporated.
Pour the final cream mixture into sterilized jars filling them to the top and allowing them to cool before capping tightly for storage.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for feverish feeling are topical products infused with plant extracts that provide relief from fever and associated discomforts. These creams work by promoting sweating, reducing inflammation, and soothing the skin. Some of the best herbal creams for feverish feeling include Echinacea purpurea, Zingiber officinale, Eucalyptus globulus, Cinchona officinalis, Ginkgo biloba, Achillea millefolium, Melaleuca alternifolia, Sambucus nigra, Glycyrrhiza glabra, and Lavandula angustifolia. If left untreated, feverish feelings can lead to irritability, fatigue, and decreased productivity, impacting daily life and relationships.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to feverish feeling that are treated with herbal creams are conditions such as acne, rosacea, eczema, and minor burns. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile help soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and relieving discomfort. These natural remedies also promote healing and prevent infection, providing effective relief from itching, redness, and swelling associated with these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with feverish feeling is a blend of Peppermint, Eucalyptus, and Tea Tree oil creams. Peppermint cream cools the body, while Eucalyptus cream helps to reduce congestion and ease breathing. Tea Tree oil cream possesses antiviral and antibacterial properties, which aid in fighting off underlying infections. Together, these creams provide a soothing and holistic approach to alleviate feverish feelings, promoting relaxation and comfort. They can be applied topically to the skin or added to a warm bath for maximum benefits.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with feverish feeling because they contain bioactive compounds that stimulate the immune system, reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. The cream's antiviral and antibacterial properties help to alleviate symptoms of fever, such as sore throats and body aches. The anti-inflammatory properties of Echinacea also help to calm the body and mind, promoting relaxation and a sense of well-being. This natural remedy can provide relief from feverish feelings, promoting a swift recovery.
remedy_constituents: Cichoric acid: It helps reduce inflammation and fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.
Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help alleviate fever and reduce inflammation by modulating the immune response and inhibiting the release of histamine.
Caffeic acid: It has anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, helping to reduce fever and alleviate related symptoms by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used to make creams due to their high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Flowers are used to make creams because they contain alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, which have immune-boosting and anti-inflammatory effects.
Roots: Roots are used to make creams due to their high content of glycosides, phenolic acids, and alkylamides, which have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried echinacea purpurea flowers in late summer when petals have faded and flowers have turned a light brown color.
Steep the dried flowers in 500ml of boiling distilled water for 10 minutes to create a strong herbal infusion.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix 50g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat until the mixture is fully melted and smooth.
Combine the cooled herbal infusion with the melted wax and oil mixture and whip the mixture until it has doubled in volume.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with feverish feeling because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. When applied topically, gingerol is absorbed through the skin, helping to reduce body heat and alleviate discomfort associated with fever. Its warming and soothing effects can also ease congestion and cough, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. Additionally, ginger has natural analgesic properties that can help relieve aching muscles and joints.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Help reduce fever by inducing sweating, which cools the body down and relieves fever.
Shogaols: Have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate body aches and pain associated with fever, thereby providing relief.
Terpenes (specifically, borneol and geraniol): Act as a natural antipyretic, helping to lower the body temperature and reduce the severity of fever.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the ginger plant are used to make creams that help to reduce fever and soothe the body due to their rich content of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties.

Leaves: The leaves of the ginger plant are also used in some creams for feverish feeling, as they contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of fever.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 tablespoon of dried zingiber officinale root in 2 cups of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or fine mesh into a clean container.
Combine 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add the infused liquid to the coconut oil and beeswax mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let cool and solidify for about 30 minutes to an hour.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with feverish feeling because of its potent decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in Eucalyptus globulus, such as eucalyptol, work to reduce inflammation and ease congestion in the body. This helps to cool down the body temperature, providing relief from the discomfort and fatigue associated with fever. Additionally, Eucalyptus globulus creams promote relaxation and alleviate respiratory issues, making them a popular natural remedy for feverish feelings. They also help to improve the overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol: It helps with feverish feeling by acting as a natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and congestion in the respiratory tract.
Cineole: It helps with feverish feeling by exerting analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, providing relief from headaches and body aches associated with fever.
α-Pinene: It helps with feverish feeling by exhibiting antiseptic and expectorant properties, helping to clear mucus from the lungs and reduce the severity of respiratory symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Eucalyptus globulus leaves are commonly used in creams for feverish feelings because they contain eucalyptol, a natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory agent.
Barks: Eucalyptus globulus barks are often used in creams for feverish feelings due to their high content of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin that helps reduce pain and inflammation.
Oils from the leaves:  Also known as Eucalyptol,  eucalyptus globulus  leaf oil is used in creams for its  decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties which help alleviate feverish feelings.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of eucalyptus globulus leaves, fresh or dried, and clean them thoroughly.
Infuse 20g of the eucalyptus globulus leaves in 100ml of carrier oil, such as sweet almond oil.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks, shaking the bottle daily, in a cool dark place.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Mix the infused oil with 10g of beeswax, 10g of shea butter, and 20g of coconut oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams helps with feverish feeling because they contain quinine, a naturally occurring compound that has been used for centuries to combat malaria and reduce fever. The active ingredients in the cream, such as quinidine and quinidine epoxide, have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which help to soothe the body and bring down a fever. The cream can be applied topically to the skin, allowing the quinine compounds to be absorbed, providing relief from feverish sensations and promoting a sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Quinic acid: It helps reduce fever by its antipyretic properties, which inhibit the production of pyrogens and decrease the body temperature.
Quinine: It has antipyretic and analgesic effects, helping to alleviate feverish feelings by blocking the action of prostaglandins and reducing inflammation.
Quinovic acid: It has been found to have antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its ability to reduce fever and alleviate associated discomforts.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make creams due to their high content of quinine, an alkaloid that helps reduce fever and alleviate symptoms.
Barks: Used to make creams due to their rich content of quinine and other alkaloids that have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties.
Stems: Used to make creams due to their content of quinine and other alkaloids that help reduce fever and relieve symptoms associated with feverish conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of dried cinchona officinalis bark from a reputable supplier or your garden.
Steep the cinchona officinalis bark in 500ml of cold pressed olive oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discard solids.
Mix the infused oil with 20g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and emulsifies forming a smooth herbal cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with feverish feeling because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation and cool the body, alleviating the discomfort associated with fever. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's natural vasodilatory effects can help to improve blood circulation, which can aid in the removal of heat and toxins from the body. This can help to reduce the severity and duration of feverish feelings, promoting a sense of relief and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help reduce fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to the development of fever.
Bilobalide: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate feverish feeling by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.

Ginkgolides: These sesquiterpene lactones have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help reduce fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and reducing the severity of viral infections.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, making them suitable for feverish creams.
Barks: The barks of the Ginkgo biloba tree contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which are thought to help reduce fever and alleviate associated symptoms.
(Optional) Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which can also be applied to feverish creams.
remedy_recipe: Measure 30 grams of ginkgo biloba dried leaves and 20 grams of beeswax in separate containers.
Combine 50 grams of coconut oil and 30 grams of shea butter in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture from step 2 at 60-70°c for 30-40 minutes then set aside.
Mix the ginkgo biloba leaves and beeswax in a separate bowl then add 10 grams of vitamin e oil.
Combine the heated mixture from step 3 with the ginkgo biloba mixture and stir until smooth consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium creams helps with feverish feeling because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb, commonly known as yarrow, contains compounds that have a cooling effect on the skin, which can help to alleviate the heat and discomfort associated with a fever. By applying the cream topically, the active ingredients can be absorbed into the skin, providing relief from feverish sensations and promoting a sense of calmness and comfort. This natural remedy can be a gentle and effective way to manage feverish feelings.
remedy_constituents: Essential oils (especially bornyl acetate and camphor): These compounds help to reduce fever by promoting sweating, which helps to cool the body and relieve discomfort associated with fever.
Flavonoids (especially apigenin and luteolin): These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate feverish symptoms.
Polyacetylenes (especially achillein): These compounds have been shown to possess antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce fever and alleviate associated symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in essential oils, particularly camphor and borneol, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Flowers: They contain sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic effects.
Stems: They are a good source of essential oils, including camphor and borneol, which can help reduce fever and alleviate discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium flowers and leaves in the morning when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to preserve its properties.
Combine 1 cup of dried achillea millefolium with 1 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 2 hours while stirring occasionally to infuse the oil with the plant's active compounds.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth and mix it with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to create a stable cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with feverish feeling because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea tree oil contained within these creams has natural cooling effects, which can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with fever. Additionally, its antiseptic properties can aid in fighting off underlying infections that may be contributing to the fever, promoting a faster recovery and relief from symptoms. This natural remedy can be a gentle yet effective solution for alleviating feverish feelings.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: These flavonoids help with feverish feelings by possessing anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which aid in reducing the severity of fever and associated symptoms.
Cineol (Eucalyptol): This monoterpene acts as a decongestant and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to relieve congestion and reduce fever through its cooling and soothing effects on the skin.
Cymene: A monoterpenic compound with antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, cymene helps alleviate feverish feelings by reducing inflammation and combating the underlying causes of fever.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most used part of Melaleuca alternifolia to make creams for feverish feelings because they contain the active compound cineole, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Buds: Buds are also used to make creams for feverish feelings as they contain oils that help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms.

Leaves: The leaves are often combined with the oil from the buds, making the leaves another key part of the Melaleuca alternifolia used in creams for feverish feelings due to their high cineole content.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 200g of base cream in a stainless steel mixing bowl to create a smooth consistency.
Add 20g of melaleuca alternifolia essential oil to the base cream and mix thoroughly for 2 minutes.
Stir 10g of vitamin e oil into the mixture to enhance its moisturizing properties slowly.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60-70 degrees celsius for 10-15 minutes to ensure uniformity.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and allow it to cool completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra creams helps with feverish feeling because they contain bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids found in the elderberry plant. These compounds have natural anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help to reduce the severity and duration of fever. They also work to boost the immune system, helping the body to fight off the underlying cause of the fever. By reducing inflammation and promoting immune function, Sambucus nigra creams can provide relief from feverish feelings.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help reduce fever by acting as antipyretics and anti-inflammatory agents, thereby alleviating the discomfort and pain associated with fever.
Phenylethylamine: Contribute to the reduction of fever by inducing a cooling effect in the body, thereby lowering the body temperature.
Catechins: Exhibit antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate the symptoms of fever, such as headaches, fatigue, and body aches.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antiviral and anti-inflammatory compounds, they help soothe and calm the body.
Leaves: Containing flavonoids and terpenoids, they aid in reducing fever and inflammation.
Stems: With their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, they help combat infections and alleviate discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried sambucus nigra flowers and 20g of beeswax for future use in the recipe.
Combine 30g of dried sambucus nigra flowers with 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture for 2 hours then strain it through a cheesecloth to obtain the infused oil.
Mix 20g of beeswax with 100g of shea butter and 20g of the infused oil in a bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat then pour it into a container to cool and solidify the cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with feverish feeling because they contain licorice root extract, which has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The flavonoids and saponins present in licorice root help to soothe and calm the body, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with fever. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra has been traditionally used to support the immune system and relieve symptoms of colds and flu, making it a popular natural remedy for alleviating feverish feelings. Its cooling and calming effects help to provide relief and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These triterpenoid compounds help to reduce inflammation and fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and inducing the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines.
Flavonoids: These phenolic compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the severity of fever by protecting the body from oxidative stress and inflammation.

Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with feverish feeling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and inducing the production of anti-inflammatory mediators.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high glycyrrhizin content, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which possess antioxidant and fever-reducing properties.
Barks: The barks are used due to their ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, helping to reduce fever and alleviate discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Extract 30g of glycyrrhiza glabra root in 500ml of cold water and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture and collect the liquid to use in the cream base preparation.
Mix 10g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter with the extracted liquid in a double boiler.
Add 10g of distilled water and 1g of potassium sorbate to the mixture and heat it to 80c.
Pour the mixture into sterilized containers and let it cool and solidify for 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with feverish feeling because they possess calming and soothing properties. The essential oils present in these creams, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have a cooling effect on the skin, which can help reduce body temperature. Additionally, the sedative properties of lavender oil can promote relaxation and help alleviate restlessness associated with fever. This can lead to improved sleep and a reduced sense of discomfort, ultimately helping to alleviate the symptoms of a fever.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the nervous system, which can alleviate feverish feelings by promoting relaxation and calmness.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to ease muscle tension and discomfort associated with fever.
Lavandulol: Possesses a calming effect on the nervous system and has antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety caused by fever.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used for their calming and antiseptic properties, which help reduce fever and soothe the skin.
Leaves: Employed for their ability to promote sweating, which aids in releasing the body's toxins and reducing fever.
Stems: Utilized for their antimicrobial properties, which help prevent infection and promote healing in skin affected by fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and store them in a cool dark place for 2 hours.
Combine 100g of beeswax and 50g of shea butter in a double boiler and heat for 30 minutes.
Add 100g of coconut oil and the dried lavandula angustifolia flowers to the double boiler and heat for 10 minutes.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let the mixture infuse for 4 hours then strain it.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and set for 2 hours.
